<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ikasuri Shrine]][[fr:Ikasuri-jinja]][[ja:坐摩神社]][[simple:Ikasuri Shrine]][[zh:坐摩神社]]
{
{
{{Short description|Shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Multiple issues|
{{more citations needed|date=November 2023}}
{{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=坐摩神社|date=December 2021}}
}}
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Ikasuri-jinja, keidai.jpg|deity={{ill|Ikasuri|qid=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}}|location=Watanabe-3, Kyūtarō-machi, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|qid=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}} City, Osaka, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}|prefecture={{ill|Kyūtarō-machi|qid=Q60996434|1=Kyūtarō-machi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.680833|N|135.498611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}}}}

'''Ikasuri Shrine''' (坐摩神社, ''Ikasuri jinja'', also known as ''Zama jinja'') is a major [[Shinto]] shrine located in central {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}, Japan. Its annual festival is on April 22. In the former [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] it was an imperial shrine of the second Rank or ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]'' (官幣中社). It was also the [[ichinomiya]] of the former {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}.
It enshrines five [[kami]] known as ''ikasuri no kami'' or ''zama no kami'': Ikui no kami (生井神), Sakui no kami (福井神), Tsunagai no kami (綱長井神), Hahiki no kami (波比祇神), and Asuha no kami (阿須波神); collectively known as {{nihongo|Ikasuri no Kami|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|1=Ikasuri no Kami}}

== History ==
It was founded by {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} when she returned from the Three Han (三韓, Korea) campaign.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Shinto shrines {{!}} Japan {{!}} Rods Shinto |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/ |access-date=2023-04-04 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref>

She was nearly shipwrecked but managed to survive thanks to praying to {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}, and she made the shrine to honor him.<ref name=":1" />  [[Ikuta Shrine]] and [[Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|Watatsumi Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name=":1" />

==Ikasuri no Kami==
{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| deity_of = 
| member_of = 
| alt = 
| caption = 
| other_names = 
| hiero = 
| avatar_birth = 
| avatar_end = 
| script_name = 
| script = [[Japanese language|Japanese]]
| consorts = 
| father = [[Toshigami]]
| mother = {{nihongo|Amechikarumizu-hime|天知迦流美豆比売|}}
| siblings = {{ill|Hahiki-no-kami|ja|波比岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hahiki-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10928586}}
| offspring = 
| region = 
| festivals = 
| name = Asuha-no-kami
}}
Two of the goddesses composing Ikasuri no Kami: Asuha-no-kami and Hahiki-no-kami are daughters of [[Toshigami]]{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| deity_of = 
| member_of = 
| alt = 
| caption = 
| other_names = 
| hiero = 
| avatar_birth = 
| avatar_end = 
| script_name = 
| script = [[Japanese language|Japanese]]
| consorts = 
| father = [[Toshigami]]
| mother = {{nihongo|Amechikarumizu-hime|天知迦流美豆比売|}}
| siblings = {{ill|Asuha-no-kami|ja|阿須波神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuha-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10928586}}
| offspring = 
| region = 
| festivals = 
| name = Hahiki-no-kami
}}

==See also==
*[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]]

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons Category|Ikasuri-jinja}}
*[http://www.ikasuri.or.jp Official website]

<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->

{{coord|34.68096|135.49862|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}}

{{Munakata Faith}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{translated page|ja|坐摩神社|version=101361653|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[en:Ikasuri Shrine]]
[[ja:坐摩神社]]

<!-- for alternate text of the title image per [[WP:ALT]] --><!-- for alternate text of the title image per [[WP:ALT]] -->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Ikasuri-jinja}}
[[fr:Ikasuri-jinja]]
[[simple:Ikasuri Shrine]]
[[zh:坐摩神社]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Ikasuri-jinja}}
[[fr:Ikasuri-jinja]]
[[simple:Ikasuri Shrine]]
[[zh:坐摩神社]]
<!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q11425667}}
[[Category:Beppyo shrines]]
[[Category:beppyo shrines]]
[[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Osaka]]
[[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]]
[[Category:Empress Jingū]]
[[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]]
[[Category:Ichinomiya]]
[[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]]
[[Category:Settsu Province]]
[[Category:Shikinai Taisha]]
[[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
[[Category:Watanabe Clan of Settsu Province]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]]
[[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]]
[[Category:Shikinai Taisha]]
[[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]]
[[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
